senses.definition: a span of time which we do not work or go to school, and spend traveling or resting instead, particularly in a different city, country, etc.

examples.x: We went on a vacation to Hawaii last summer.
My family is planning a vacation to Europe next month.
After working hard for months, I finally took a vacation to the mountains.
They are going on a vacation to the beach for two weeks.
I need a vacation to relax and recharge my batteries.

senses.definition: an exciting or unusual experience, often involving risk or physical activity

examples.x: They embarked on a thrilling adventure, exploring remote jungles and scaling towering peaks.
Riding the rapids in the whitewater rafting expedition was an adrenaline-fueled adventure.
Her solo trek through the desert was a test of endurance and self-discovery, a true adventure.
The children's book series follows the young protagonists on magical adventures in faraway lands.
They planned a camping trip in the wilderness, craving the freedom and excitement of outdoor adventure.

senses.definition: the act of travelling between two or more places, especially when there is a long distance between them

examples.x: Their journey across the country took them through diverse landscapes and cultures.
The long journey by train provided ample time for reflection and introspection.
She documented her solo journey through Europe in a travel journal, capturing memories and experiences along the way.
The family embarked on a road trip, making stops at various landmarks and attractions during their journey.
The journey to the summit of the mountain tested their physical endurance and mental resilience.

senses.definition: a journey taken by a ship for pleasure, especially one involving several destinations

examples.x: They booked a Caribbean cruise for their honeymoon, eager to explore tropical islands and relax on luxurious cruise liners.
She enjoyed the onboard entertainment and dining options during her cruise to Alaska, admiring the stunning scenery from the deck.
The river cruise took passengers through picturesque villages and historic landmarks along the Rhine River.
He saved up for years to afford a round-the-world cruise, fulfilling his dream of circumnavigating the globe by sea.
The cruise director organized daily activities and events to keep passengers entertained during the transatlantic crossing.

senses.definition: a journey that you take for fun or a particular reason, generally for a short amount of time

examples.x: The family planned a trip to the beach for their summer vacation.
He packed his bags for a week-long business trip to attend a conference.
Every year, the Smith family takes a trip to visit relatives in another state.
She went on a quick shopping trip to the mall to pick up some essentials.

senses.definition: related or belonging to a country or region other than your own

examples.x: Watching foreign films provides viewers with a glimpse into the storytelling and cinematic styles of different cultures.
Trying foreign foods allows you to savor flavors and dishes from different parts of the world.
She studied foreign languages such as French and German in school.
The company expanded its operations into foreign markets in Asia.

senses.definition: a person who lives in a country where they are not a citizen or permanent resident

examples.x: As a foreigner, she had to adjust to local customs.
The law applies equally to citizens and foreigners.
Tourists and foreigners often visit that historic site.
He felt like a foreigner despite living there for years.
Being a foreigner in a new country can be both exciting and challenging.

senses.definition: a hotel near the road suitable for people who are on a road trip, usually with rooms arranged in a row and parking places outside

examples.x: They stopped at a roadside motel for the night during their cross-country road trip, grateful for a comfortable place to rest.
She checked into a quaint motel on the outskirts of town, charmed by its retro decor and friendly staff.
The family stayed at a pet-friendly motel, allowing them to bring their furry companion along on their journey.
The motel offered complimentary breakfast and Wi-Fi, catering to the needs of modern travelers.

senses.definition: the act of stopping a planned event from happening or an order for something from being completed

examples.x: The flight faced a cancelation due to severe weather conditions.
The concert's cancelation disappointed many fans who had bought tickets.
I received a notification about the cancelation of my reservation at the restaurant.

senses.definition: the act of arranging something, such as a seat or a hotel room to be kept for you to use later at a particular time

examples.x: I made a reservation at the restaurant for dinner tonight to celebrate my sister's birthday.
The couple secured their seats on the train by making a reservation online before the journey.
Due to high demand, we were unable to get a reservation at our favorite cafe for Sunday brunch.
The travel agency helped us arrange reservations for guided tours and excursions during our vacation abroad.
His reservation was canceled due to a payment issue.

senses.definition: to arrange something to be kept for later use

examples.x: They reserved a table at the restaurant for their anniversary dinner.
She reserved a hotel room online before her trip to ensure accommodation upon arrival.
He reserved a rental car for the weekend getaway to explore the countryside.
They reserved tickets to the concert months in advance to secure their attendance.
The company reserved seats for the conference attendees, ensuring everyone had a place to sit.

senses.definition: an official mark on someone's passport that allows them to enter or stay in a country

examples.x: She obtained a work visa to live and work in Australia for a year.
They were granted a student visa to attend university in the United States.
The embassy issued a business visa to the entrepreneur for a conference in Japan.
He traveled to the consulate to renew his visa before it expired.

senses.definition: to live somewhere for a short time, especially as a guest or visitor

examples.x: They invited us to stay at their beach house for the weekend.
I'm staying at a friend's apartment while I'm in town for the conference.
He's visiting from out of town and needs a place to stay for the weekend.
Due to the storm, we couldn't drive home and had to stay overnight at a motel.
My friend is coming to stay with me next week.

senses.definition: ‌a card that usually has a picture on one side, used for sending messages by post without an envelope

examples.x: She sent a postcard from her vacation, featuring a stunning beach sunset on the front.
They collect vintage postcards as a hobby, fascinated by the historical images and messages.
He wrote a brief message on the back of the postcard before dropping it in the mailbox.
The souvenir shop sold postcards featuring iconic landmarks and attractions of the city.
She received a postcard from her pen pal abroad, eagerly reading about their adventures.

senses.definition: something that we usually buy and bring back for other people from a place that we have visited on vacation

examples.x: He found a handcrafted wooden figurine as the perfect souvenir of his visit to the mountain village.
She collected seashells from the beach as souvenirs of her seaside vacation.
They purchased miniature Eiffel Tower replicas as souvenirs for their coworkers after their trip to France.
She bought a keychain souvenir from the gift shop to remember her trip to Paris.
They picked up some local chocolates as souvenirs to share with friends and family back home.

senses.definition: to go somewhere for a short time, especially to see something

examples.x: During their vacation, they planned to visit famous landmarks and historical sites in the city.
They decided to visit the museum to admire the new art exhibit.
The tourists visited the zoo to see exotic animals from around the world.
He made it a point to visit the beach at least once a month to relax and unwind.
They were excited to visit the theme park and experience the thrilling rides and attractions.

senses.definition: to visit interesting and well-known places

examples.x: Tourists often come to the city to sightsee and experience its cultural landmarks.
During their vacation, the family plans to sightsee in historic districts and museums.
The family is currently sightseeing in the city's famous landmarks.
Last summer, the group sightseed along the historical sites.

senses.definition: to confirm your presence or reservation in a hotel or airport after arriving

examples.x: We'll check in as soon as we reach the hotel.
Make sure to check in at the airport well before your flight.
We usually check in online to save time.

senses.definition: to leave a hotel after returning your room key and paying the bill
in or traveling to a different country

examples.x: They plan to travel abroad next summer to explore Europe.
He went abroad for business and returned with new ideas.
Many students study abroad to experience different cultures.
We usually go abroad for a week in May to escape the routine.
The company sent several employees abroad for the conference.

senses.definition: to move from a vehicle, airplane, etc. to another in order to continue a journey

examples.x: Passengers will need to change at the next station.
We had to change in Chicago to reach our final destination.
You'll need to change at the bus terminal to continue your journey.
You'll need to change in London to catch your connecting flight.

senses.definition: to travel or cross something in an aircraft

examples.x: The adventurous couple decided to fly to Europe for their honeymoon.
In order to attend an important conference, the executive had to fly to the corporate headquarters.
Instead of a long road trip, the family chose to fly to their dream destination.
Due to the urgency of the situation, the diplomat had to fly to the crisis-stricken region.
The famous band planned to fly to various countries as part of their world tour.

senses.definition: to arrive and rest on the ground or another surface after being in the air

examples.x: The plane lands smoothly on the runway.
The bird lands on the branch to rest.
The helicopter is landing on the rooftop.
The skydivers have landed after their thrilling jump.

senses.definition: to leave a surface and begin flying

examples.x: The airplane is ready to take off from the runway.
Pilots need permission to take off from the airport.
Birds effortlessly take off into the sky with a flap of their wings.
The rocket ship is designed to take off vertically from the launchpad.
As the helicopter prepared to take off, the rotor blades began to spin.

senses.definition: a list or chart that shows the times at which trains, buses, planes, etc. leave and arrive

examples.x: She checked the train schedule to plan her commute to work.
They consulted the flight schedule to find the most convenient departure time for their trip.
The bus schedule indicated that the next bus would arrive in 15 minutes.
The ferry schedule outlined the departure times for trips between the islands.

senses.definition: to move in a relaxed or casual manner

examples.x: I wandered through the narrow streets, enjoying the sights and sounds of the city.
She wandered around the park, lost in thought as the autumn leaves crunched beneath her feet.
They spent the afternoon wandering through the art museum, admiring the paintings at their own pace.
He wandered along the beach, listening to the waves and feeling the sand between his toes.
I wandered through the narrow streets of the old town, stopping occasionally to admire the architecture.
As the evening breeze picked up, they wandered along the riverbank, chatting idly and enjoying the cool air.

senses.definition: the act of arriving at a place from somewhere else

examples.x: The train’s arrival was announced on the station’s loudspeaker.
His unexpected arrival at the party surprised everyone.
She eagerly awaited the arrival of her friends at the airport.
The arrival of the train was announced over the loudspeaker.

senses.definition: the act of leaving, usually to begin a journey

examples.x: The departure of the train was delayed by half an hour.
She bid farewell to her family before her departure for college.
The departure of the ship from the harbor marked the beginning of their cruise vacation.
They gathered at the airport to see off their friend before her departure for overseas.
He packed his bags in anticipation of his departure for the backpacking trip.

senses.definition: the place at an airport or port where passengers' bags are checked for illegal goods as they enter a country

examples.x: They passed through customs quickly, as they had nothing to declare.
She declared the souvenirs she bought abroad to the customs officials.
They were stopped by customs for carrying fruits across the border.
There was a long line at customs, with travelers waiting to have their passports stamped.
They waited in line at customs for over an hour after their flight.

senses.definition: a journey on a horse, bicycle, automobile, or machine

examples.x: She enjoyed a peaceful ride through the countryside on her horse, savoring the fresh air and scenic views.
The family decided to go for a bike ride along the picturesque trails in the national park.
After a long day of hiking, they hitched a ride back to the campground with a friendly passerby.
The taxi ride to the airport was smooth and efficient, allowing them to arrive in time for their flight.

senses.definition: to go away from somewhere

examples.x: She left her friends at the party without any goodbye.
She decided to leave the party because it was too crowded.
The bus will leave in five minutes, so be quick!
They will leave for their vacation tomorrow morning.
I need to leave for the airport in an hour.

senses.definition: to reach a location, particularly as an end to a journey

examples.x: After a long flight, we finally arrived in Paris.
The train is scheduled to arrive at the station in just a few minutes.
The delivery truck is expected to arrive at our doorstep by noon with the package.
The bus will arrive at the terminal shortly.
We left early to ensure we would arrive at the concert venue before the performance began.

senses.definition: to decide or tell that something arranged before will now not happen

examples.x: They had to cancel their vacation plans due to unexpected work commitments.
The event was canceled at the last minute due to inclement weather.
He canceled the reservation at the restaurant because they were running late.
They canceled the meeting to give everyone more time to prepare.
The flight was canceled due to mechanical issues with the aircraft.

senses.definition: to get to your planned destination

examples.x: The beach can only be reached by boat.
The pyramids can be reached by public transport.
They didn't reach the border until after dark.
We finally reached our destination.
We reached London late at night.
